expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: shipment checkpoint covers OK or Cancel semantics, pending selection state, and dismissal correctness for multi-selection workflows
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the output explicitly enumerates the required focus areas under “This checkpoint-enforcement case requires that Phase 5b explicitly covers shipment-readiness checkpoint review for: - OK vs Cancel semantics - Pending selection state - Dismissal correctness for multi-selection workflows”, and reiterates coverage via fixture-derived statements like “Implement an “OK” button to confirm selection” and “popover may dismiss unexpectedly if selection is still loading.”

expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase5b
expectations.evidence: result.md is framed as “Phase 5b / checkpoint enforcement,” includes a “Phase 5b contract alignment” section, and references phase5b-required artifacts and routing: “Required outputs for Phase 5b: context/checkpoint_audit_<feature-id>.md, context/checkpoint_delta_<feature-id>.md, drafts/qa_plan_phase5b_r<round>.md” and the required disposition “accept / return phase5a / return phase5b.” execution_notes.md also states the assessment was limited to “whether the Phase 5b checkpoint model… cover the required semantics focus.”
